entries.d: a machine that plays music loudly
entries.s: We turned on the loud _____ for the party so everyone could hear the music.

entries.d: a machine that shows pictures on a wall
entries.s: The teacher used the _____ to show a film on the white wall screen.

entries.d: a flat surface for writing in class
entries.s: The teacher wrote the date on the _____ with a piece of white chalk.

entries.d: glass in the wall to look outside
entries.s: I can see the garden through the open _____. Birds are flying outside.

entries.d: you open it to go into a room
entries.s: Please knock on the _____ before you come in and enter the room.

entries.d: a table for reading and writing
entries.s: My books and pens are on my _____ where I do my homework.

entries.d: a tool for cutting paper
entries.s: I need my _____ to cut out this picture.

entries.d: a tool for drawing straight lines
entries.s: Use a _____ to draw a straight line.

entries.d: a small flat computer with a screen
entries.s: She plays games on her touchscreen _____ after school. She taps the glass screen.

entries.d: you sit on it
entries.s: Please sit down on your _____ at your desk during the lesson.

entries.d: a bag for your school things
entries.s: I put all my books, pens and lunch into my _____ every morning before leaving.

entries.d: you write with it, it uses ink
entries.s: Can I borrow your blue _____ to write my name in ink on the form?

entries.d: a small book for writing in class
entries.s: Write the answers to the homework in your _____ with your name on the front.

entries.d: a small bag for pens and pencils
entries.s: My pens, pencils and ruler are in my small _____ inside my school bag.

entries.d: you write with it, you can rub it out
entries.s: I drew a picture of a cat with my _____ and then rubbed out a mistake.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with PENCIL?

entries.d: you use it to rub out pencil marks
entries.s: I wrote a wrong word with my pencil, so I used my _____ to erase it.

entries.d: you read it, it has many pages
entries.s: I am reading a very funny story _____ with 200 pages and a picture on the cover.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with BOOK?

entries.d: you wear it in your hair
entries.s: She put a pink _____ around her long hair to keep it out of her face.

entries.d: dark glasses for sunny days
entries.s: It is very sunny outside, so I need my dark _____ to protect my eyes.

entries.d: you wear it on your head
entries.s: He wears a warm woolly _____ on his head in winter to keep his ears warm.

entries.d: a tie you wear with a school uniform
entries.s: Tom wears a blue _____ around his neck with his white school shirt.

entries.d: clothes you wear on the top of your body
entries.s: He put on a clean white _____ with buttons and a collar for school.

entries.d: a warm top with long sleeves
entries.s: It is cold today, so wear a thick woolly _____ over your shirt.

entries.d: girls and women wear it, it is not trousers
entries.s: She is wearing a red knee-length _____ today with tights underneath.

entries.d: you wear them on your feet inside shoes
entries.s: I put on my clean _____ on my feet and then my shoes every morning.

entries.d: you wear it on your foot
entries.s: I lost one _____ and I am hopping on one foot to find the other.

entries.d: to put something in someone's hand
entries.s: Please _____ me your pen for a moment and I will hand it back soon.

entries.d: what a clock shows
entries.s: What _____ is it? It's three o'clock in the afternoon.

entries.d: to know what something means
entries.s: I don't _____ this word. What does it mean in German?

entries.d: to put words on paper
entries.s: Please _____ your full name at the top of the paper with a pen.

entries.d: to like something very much
entries.s: I really _____ playing football with my friends and always laugh a lot.

entries.d: to use your ears to hear something
entries.s: Please _____ carefully to the teacher now and don't talk.

entries.d: to like very, very much
entries.s: I _____ my dog very much. He is my best friend in the whole world.

entries.d: a bigger number or amount of something
entries.s: Can I have _____ water in my glass, please? Mine is empty.

entries.d: to look at words and understand them
entries.s: I like to _____ story books with interesting words before I go to sleep.

entries.d: belonging to them
entries.s: The children put _____ bags on the floor next to their own chairs.

entries.d: to say a question to someone
entries.s: I want to _____ the teacher a question about my homework.

entries.d: where someone lives or an email name
entries.s: What is your email _____? I want to send you a message online.

entries.d: a question about how someone feels
entries.s: Hello, Tom! _____ 'I'm fine, thanks' is my usual reply.

entries.d: I feel good
entries.s: How are you? _____ Thank you!

entries.d: to see someone for the first time
entries.s: Nice to _____ you for the first time! My name is Anna.

entries.d: after that, next
entries.s: First, read the text. _____ answer the questions at the bottom.

entries.d: belonging to you
entries.s: Is this _____ pencil or is it mine? It was on your desk.

entries.d: to use your eyes to see something
entries.s: _____ at this beautiful picture on the wall! Can you see the colours?

entries.d: a word to give a choice between two things
entries.s: Do you want tea _____ milk with your cake? You choose one.

entries.d: to put food in your mouth
entries.s: I am very hungry. I want to _____ a sandwich with ham and cheese.

entries.d: to move from one place to another
entries.s: I _____ to school every morning at eight o'clock on foot.

entries.d: to have to do something
entries.s: It is late. I _____ go home now.

entries.d: asking about the number of something
entries.s: _____ cats do you have at home? I have three.

entries.d: to not like at all
entries.s: I really _____ getting up early in the morning. It is the worst part of my day.

entries.d: in this place
entries.s: Come _____ to me! I want to show you something on my phone.

entries.d: a word for a thing or animal
entries.s: I have a pet cat. _____ is black and white and has long whiskers.

entries.d: a word to suggest doing something together
entries.s: _____ go to the park together after lunch and play football!

entries.d: twelve o'clock at night
entries.s: It is _____, exactly 12 o'clock at night, and everyone is fast asleep.

entries.d: belonging to us
entries.s: This is _____ classroom where all of us in class 1A learn together.

entries.d: the one you like the most
entries.s: Blue is my _____ colour — I love it more than any other colour in the world.

entries.d: to see something you were looking for
entries.s: I can't _____ my red pen anywhere. Have you seen it?

entries.d: not dark, a pale colour
entries.s: Her eyes are _____ blue, almost the colour of the sky on a sunny day.

entries.d: a young person, a boy or a girl
entries.s: Every _____ in the class — Tom, Anna, Max — got a new book from the teacher.

entries.d: to make something not dirty
entries.s: Please _____ the dirty board with the cloth after the lesson.

entries.d: to shut something
entries.s: It is cold in here. Please _____ the open window to keep the warm air in.

entries.d: to make something not closed
entries.s: Please _____ your closed books now and turn to page ten.

entries.d: a drawing, painting, or photo
entries.s: She drew a beautiful _____ of her family with coloured pencils on paper.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with PICTURE?

entries.d: to move your body onto a chair
entries.s: Please _____ on your chair and be quiet for the lesson.

entries.d: to say words, to talk
entries.s: Please don't _____ during the test. Keep quiet and write your answers.

entries.d: to get up from a chair
entries.s: Please _____ from your chair when the teacher comes into the room.

entries.d: to get something from inside a bag
entries.s: Please _____ your exercise books from your school bags and put them on your desks.

entries.d: a group of students who learn together
entries.s: There are twenty children in our _____ 1A. Our teacher is Mrs. Smith.
